type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Higher number of blasts. Change in quantification methodology - from emission factors to source testing results for several point sources (electrowinning, smelting, carbon regeneration kiln). Increase from previous 2019 due to a change in the amount of stationary fuel combusted (see Stationary Combustion Tab). NO2 was the most affected.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Air - fugitives/material handling speciated from TPM - additional dust controls applied to the crushing circuit & change in quantification methods from emission factors sourced from Australian NPI to US EPA AP-42 (crushing/screening).
Water - increase in volume of effluent released and increase in arsenic concentration in effluent.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: There was much less ore blasting in 2023, when compared with 2022, and the stationary combustion was also slightly reduced. It is just below the reporting threshold.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: There was much less ore blasting in 2023, when compared with 2022, but there was more stationary combustion. For Carbon Monoxide, the primary source was blasting, and now it is below NPRI reporting thresholds.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 70% via discharge into Scraggy Lake (58% in 2022). Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2021. Annual concentration decreased by approximatetly 4%. The mining operations halted in late 2023. Previously, 31% of emissions came from the mine, but that has fallen to 17%. Since the effluent has remained fairly constant, there is less total Arsenic reaching the environment.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings, as well as waste rock. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with 4% less recirculated to the mill. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had increased by 18%, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 25%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 6%. 

Waste rock quantities decreased by 97% in 2023.

Effectively, in the tailings, there were less solids, and there was almost no waste rock (when compared to 2022). Therefore, despite increased tailings liquid portions, there was a net decrease in Arsenic disposed of.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was about 25% less in 2023.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had increased by 30%, and also an increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 13%. In the solids portion, the concentration remained the same. 

Effectively, Cadmium concentrations increased in both the liquid inlet and outlet, but more so on the inlet side. Despite the increase in flow, there was less overall tailings solids.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings, as well as waste rock. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had increased by 103%, and also an increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 33%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 10%. 

Waste rock quantities decreased by 97% in 2023.

Effectively, in the tailings, there were less solids, and there was almost no waste rock (when compared to 2022). Therefore, despite increased tailings liquid portions, there was a net decrease in Cobalt disposed of.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released to water (90% in 2022) vis discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. However, there was a 32% increase in average effluent concentration. There were higher concentration increases during months of high effluent flow (as it is broken down by month).

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
"Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximatley 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was a 50% decrease, and also a increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 22%. In the solids portion, the concentration decreased by 2%. 

Effectively, despite more flow into the TMF, the inlet concentration decreased and the concentration at the recirculation pipe increased, creating less net flow out. There were also less solids added."

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: 100% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake (same as 2022). Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was about 16% higher.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in the liquid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. The ionic cyanide is composed of multiple constituents. In general, the concentration in the flow in was approximately 5% higher, while the flow out was approximately 4% lower.

 Overall, there was more liquid flow in, and less net flow out.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 65% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake (compared to ~48% in 2022). Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was approximately 12% higher. The mining operations halted in late 2023. Previously, 40% of emissions came from the mine, but that has fallen to 19%. Since the effluent has remained fairly constant, there is less total Lead reaching the environment.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings, as well as waste rock. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had increased by 301%, and also an increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 75%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 20%. 

Waste rock quantities decreased by 97% in 2023.

Effectively, in the tailings, there were less solids, and there was almost no waste rock (when compared to 2022). Therefore, despite increased tailings liquid portions, there was a net decrease in Lead disposed of.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was 200% higher.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximatley 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was a 37% decrease, and also a increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 190%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 3%. 

Effectively, despite more liquid flow into the TMF, the inlet concentration decreased and the concnetration at the recirculation pipe increased, creating more net flow out. There were also less solids added.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: ~ 50% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake (compared to ~35% in 2022). Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was approximately 15% less. The mining operations halted in late 2023. Previously, 50% of emissions came from the mine, but that has fallen to 31%. Since the effluent has remained fairly constant, there is less total Mercury reaching the environment.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings, as well as waste rock. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was no change in inlet concentration, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 7%. In the solids portion, the concentration decreased by 16%. 

Waste rock quantities decreased by 97% in 2023.

Effectively, in the tailings, there were less solids, and there was almost no waste rock (when compared to 2022, also where mercury was most concentrated). Therefore, despite increased tailings liquid portions, there was a net decrease in Mercury disposed of.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was about 4% less on average (note, that effluent is calculated on a monthly basis).

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings, as well as small quantities moved off site in contaminated soils. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had increased by 230%, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 20%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 9%. 

Effectively, there was more flow into the TMF, but despite the increased concentration, there was still less overall mass sent to the tailings (due to solids).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was approximately 56% less.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27%. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was a concentration increase of 38%, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 48%. There was no change to the concentration in the solid tailings.

Effectively, Selenium concentrations increased in both the liquid inlet and outlet, but more so on the inlet side. Despite the increase in flow, there was less overall tailings solids.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration had decreased by 62%, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 16%. In the solids portion, the concentration increased by 3%. 

Effectively, Zinc concentrations decreased more on the liquid inlet. Despite the increase in flow, there was less overall zinc in the system, and less from solids.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: > 95% released via discharge into Scraggy Lake. Total effluent in 2023 was 3.5% less than in 2022. Annual concentration was 15% lower.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
"Disposed of in tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was an 11% increase in concentration, and also a decrease in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 19% in concentration. 

Effectively, there was more total liquid in, and about the same liquid out. The concentration in increased, and the concentration out decreased."

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in both liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27% in 2023. In the liquid sent to the TMF, the concentration was about the same, and also a increase in measured concentration in the recirculated water to the mill of approximately 20%. In the solids portion, the concentration decreased by 3%. 

Effectively, despite more liquid flow into the TMF, the inlet concentration decreased and the concnetration at the recirculation pipe increased, creating net flow out. There were also less solids added.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment:
Disposed of in liquid and solid tailings. There was a 15% increase in tailings water sent to the TMF, with approximately the same amount water pulled from the TMF. Solid tailings decreased by approximately 27%. In the liquid sent to the TMF, there was a concentration decrease of 15%, and no change in the recirculation water concentration. There was no change to the concentration in the solid tailings.

Effectively, Thallium concentrations increased in both the liquid inlet and outlet, but more so on the inlet side. Despite the increase in flow, there was less overall tailings solids.

comments.comment: Concentration of released component is down approximately 50% on an annual basis. Effluent flow is down approximately 20%. This is based on the variability of the sampled effluent, but also the facility has ceased production and has entered care and maintenance so no new tailings are generated.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: No waste rock or solid tailings were generated. Disposals were through runoff water entering into pits no longer pumped to the tailings pond. Concentrations were estimated based on samples of the pit water and the volume of water added to the pits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Although diesel combustion was higher due to additional dewatering activities & stationary pump use, the amount of rock blasted decreased in 2021 when compared to 2020. While the total number of blasts increased, the quantity or rock blasted in each decreased.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Primarily released to Water (35%, down from 42% in 2020) and Air (49%, up from 43% in 2020) - Less effluent total in 2021 (15% less than 2020). The concentration in the effluent is less compared to 2020. There were less releases from ore blasting and crushing than in 2020.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
